name: By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena
name_original: Por los Pozuelos de Calatrava y la Tabla de la Yedra — circular por Piedrabuena
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcolea De Calatrava traverse the unique volcanic landscapes of the Campo de Calatrava region in Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. This area features an ancient volcanic field with craters, lagoons, and eroded lava flows, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Roads often wind through undulating sections, agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves, with the Guadiana River also flowing through parts of the municipality. The region offers a mix of flat sections and climbs, particularly when approaching historic sites. Best road cycling routes around Alcolea De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop, a 72.1 miles (116.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the wider regional landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena, a difficult 65.0 miles (104.7 km) path. This route offers extensive views across the Calatrava plains. Local road cyclists also love the Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through volcanic landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Road cycling around Alcolea De Calatrava is defined by volcanic formations, agricultural fields, and the Guadiana River valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcolea De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcolea De Calatrava?
A: There are 18 road cycling routes around Alcolea De Calatrava listed on komoot, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's unique volcanic landscapes and agricultural fields.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcolea De Calatrava?
A: The continental Mediterranean climate of the region makes spring and autumn the most favorable seasons for road cycling. During these times, temperatures are more moderate, providing comfortable conditions to enjoy the routes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy road cycling routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 31.6 km long and takes about 1 hour 24 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant ride through volcanic landscapes and past historical sites.

Q: Which routes offer a challenging experience for advanced road cyclists?
A: For advanced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 116 km route with significant elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251026703/by-the-pozuelos-de-calatrava-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-circular-by-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 104.7 km and offers extensive views across the Calatrava plains.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: Road cycling routes in Alcolea De Calatrava offer diverse scenery, including unique volcanic landscapes with craters and lagoons, undulating roads through picturesque agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves. You'll also find sections along the Guadiana River and views of historic sites.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; for an easier ride, or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrabuena – Valbuena Bridge loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt; for a moderate challenge.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the routes?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical sights. Along the routes, you can encounter volcanic formations like the Peñarroya Volcano and various lagoons such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. Historical points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt; and ancient structures like the Roman-era Puente de Las Ovejas (Bridge of the Sheep).

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several routes pass by scenic viewpoints. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the volcanic landscape and surrounding plains.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Alcolea De Calatrava?
A: Route durations vary significantly. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes, while more difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, may take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant water features?
A: Yes, the Guadiana River traverses the municipal territory, and some routes will take you alongside it. You can also find routes that pass by lagoons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;, adding a refreshing element to your ride.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on road cycling routes in this area?
A: The terrain in Alcolea De Calatrava is varied, featuring undulating roads and climbs, especially when approaching historic sites. Elevation gains can range from around 230 meters on easier routes to over 1000 meters on the most challenging long-distance rides, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Excellent views of the Guadina and more at this time and after a rain.

Located in the central part of the province, the surroundings of Los Pozuelos de Calatrava are characterized by presenting a soft relief characteristic of the Campo de Calatrava region. It is crossed from east to west by the Guadiana River and streams such as Fresno.
Regarding its heritage, the Church of Our Lady of the Visitation stands out.

As it passes through Piedrabuena, the Bullaque River has an area of calm waters known as the Tabla de la Yedra. This is a place of recreation that is visited annually by thousands of people, being one of the most outstanding spaces equipped for bathing in the province of Ciudad Real.

Porzuna was a Roman and Visigoth settlement, and it is from these civilizations that historical remains have been found. The first settlers of the region are probably from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic according to the archaeological remains found. Most of these can be found in the Ciudad Real museum.
